nodes.name: Chicken Stir-Fry — Garlic-Ginger Skillet
nodes.description: name: Chicken Stir-Fry — Garlic-Ginger Skillet | short_description: Quick chicken and vegetable stir-fry over brown rice with a garlic-soy glaze. | meal_suggestion: Keep portions balanced (4–6 oz chic
nodes.text_for_embedding: Meal: Chicken Stir-Fry — Garlic-Ginger Skillet - name: Chicken Stir-Fry — Garlic-Ginger Skillet | short_description: Quick chicken and vegetable stir-fry over brown rice with a garlic-soy glaze. | meal_suggestion: Keep portions balanced (4–6 oz chicken, 1 cup vegetables, 1 cup cooked brown rice) and consider low-sodium soy sauce or a splash of citrus to reduce sodium. | nutrition_note: Lean protein from chicken; fiber and micronutrients from brown rice and vegetables; moderate sodium from soy sauce. Garlic provides flavor with potential health benefits. For lower sodium, use low-sodium soy sauce.

nodes.name: Teriyaki Chicken Dinner — Berry Boost Set
nodes.description: name: Teriyaki Chicken Dinner — Berry Boost Set | short_description: Teriyaki chicken with vegetables and rice; a simple, protein-rich meal with moderate carbs. Sauce adds flavor but also sodium/sugar
nodes.text_for_embedding: Meal: Teriyaki Chicken Dinner — Berry Boost Set - name: Teriyaki Chicken Dinner — Berry Boost Set | short_description: Teriyaki chicken with vegetables and rice; a simple, protein-rich meal with moderate carbs. Sauce adds flavor but also sodium/sugar; adding a berry side can boost antioxidants. | meal_suggestion: Assemble a bowl with 4–6 oz cooked chicken, 1 cup cooked rice, and 1.5–2 cups mixed vegetables. Drizzle teriyaki sauce to taste. For a Berry Boost, include a side of berries (strawberries, blueberries) to boost antioxidants. | nutrition_note: Lean protein from chicken, fiber and micronutrients from mixed vegetables, and carbohydrates from rice create a balanced plate. Teriyaki sauce adds sodium and sugars; using a lower-sodium option or reducing sauce helps. Berry Boost isn’t reflected by the listed ingredients—adding berries would increase antioxidants and fiber.

nodes.name: Tofu Veggie Curry — Smoky Bake
nodes.description: name: Tofu Veggie Curry — Smoky Bake | short_description: Smoky baked tofu with mixed vegetables in curry sauce, served over rice. | meal_suggestion: Best as a quick weeknight meal. Typical serving: 1
nodes.text_for_embedding: Meal: Tofu Veggie Curry — Smoky Bake - name: Tofu Veggie Curry — Smoky Bake | short_description: Smoky baked tofu with mixed vegetables in curry sauce, served over rice. | meal_suggestion: Best as a quick weeknight meal. Typical serving: 1 cup cooked rice with a generous portion of tofu-vegetable curry. For more protein, add beans; to reduce carbs, swap rice for cauliflower rice. Leftovers keep 3-4 days in the fridge. | nutrition_note: Plant-based protein from tofu provides essential amino acids; fiber from vegetables and rice supports fullness. Sodium may be high if you use store-bought curry sauce—consider a low-sodium option or homemade curry to reduce salt. Baking tofu with minimal oil keeps fat modest while still adding flavor.
